query: COP30 blue zone indigenous protesters forced entry clashes security November 2025 Reuters Belém summit

{"query":"COP30 blue zone indigenous protesters forced entry clashes security November 2025 Reuters Belém summit","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1678,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.reuters.com/sustainability/cop/protestors-force-their-way-into-cop30-venue-clash-with-security-2025-11-11/","title":"Protesters force their way into COP30 summit venue, clash with security | Reuters","content":"BELEM, Brazil, Nov 11 (Reuters) - Dozens of Indigenous protesters forced their way into the COP30 climate summit venue on Tuesday and clashed with security guards at the entrance to demand climate action and forest protection."},{"url":"https://news.un.org/en/story/2025/11/1166373","title":"Indigenous protesters block COP30 entrance, demand action from Brazilian Government | UN News","content":"Indigenous protesters block COP30 entrance, demand action from Brazilian Government
Around 90 Indigenous people from the Munduruku Indigenous group staged a peaceful protest early Friday, blocking the main entrance to the Blue Zone – the restricted area set aside for negotiators – at COP30 in Belém.
Protesters were directed to meet with the Minister of Indigenous Peoples, Sônia Guajajara, and the Minister of the Environment and Climate Change, Marina Silva.
Toni highlighted that COP30 has more than 900 Indigenous participants, a significant increase from the 300 registered at last year’s conference in Baku, Azerbaijan.
During a meeting held on Thursday, many Indigenous leaders described COP30 as the most inclusive climate conference they had attended."},{"url":"https://www.theguardian.com/environment/2025/nov/12/tussles-break-out-between-protesters-and-security-at-cop30-in-brazil","title":"Tussles break out between protesters and security at Cop30 in Brazil | Cop30 | The Guardian","content":"There were tussles between protesters and security guards at the Cop30 climate talks late on Tuesday night, when a group of Indigenous and non-Indigenous people stormed the conference centre in Belém.
As security and marchers clashed, Ocaña said he saw protesters and security hitting each other with small plastic bins used to hold items near secure entrances."},{"url":"https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c0l7yjgn050o","title":"Protesters break into COP30 venue in Brazil","content":"BBC journalists saw United Nations security staff running behind a line of Brazilian soldiers shouting at delegates to immediately leave the venue.
Protesters, some wearing what appeared to be traditional indigenous dress, stormed the COP30 entrance, chanting and kicking down doors, before tussling with security personnel, videos posted online showed.
It is the first time the conference is being held in Brazil, with the talks taking place in Belém on the edge of the Amazon rainforest.
Brazil's Minister of Indigenous Peoples Sonia Guajajara hailed COP30 as "historic" event, and estimated that 3,000 Indigenous peoples from around the world would be in attendance."},{"url":"https://www.lemonde.fr/en/environment/article/2025/11/12/cop30-indigenous-protesters-clash-with-security-in-belem_6747363_114.html","title":"COP30: Indigenous protesters clash with security in Belem","content":"Dozens of Indigenous protesters clashed with security guards on Tuesday, November 11, at the COP30 climate summit in Brazil, causing minor injuries in a rare incident of violence at the United Nations (UN) climate talks.
In the evening, Indigenous demonstrators and their supporters breached security barriers at the main entrance to the conference hall in Belem and scuffled with security officers there.
Calm was quickly restored and security staff used tables and chairs to barricade the entrance to the high-level "blue zone" at the heart of the conference venue.
"The Indigenous movement wanted to present its demands inside the blue zone but were not allowed in," said Joao Santiago, a professor at the Federal University of Para.
"The march, which concluded before the COP30 venue, was a legitimate, peaceful, and organized expression of popular mobilization, built through dialogue, responsibility, and collective commitment," the group said in a statement."},{"url":"https://www.reuters.com/video/watch/idRW129712112025RP1/","title":"Indigenous protesters force their way into COP30 climate summit","content":"Reutershttps://www.reuters.com · Nov 11, 2025Reutershttps://www.reuters.com · Nov 11, 20250:41Dozens of Indigenous protesters forced their way into the COP30 climate summit venue on Tuesday (November 11) and clashed with security guards at the entrance."}]}
